what_it_does: An HTML/CSS desktop mockup that renders six inbox cards for non-happy-path build outcomes (the wire failure and lifecycle states) at ui://inbox/build-review, together with a left explanatory rail, a right inspector panel, and an invariant banner.
observations: Heavy provenance header labels it as a P2 staging probe for area D / journey J3, explicitly not registered in register.json and not run through parse.py
Maps six specific return states to real backend code references (suite.py and implement.py line ranges) and event kinds
Uses a CSS grid app layout: top header, collapsible left rail, main canvas, footer, and right panel
Each returned card has a left spine color, a stamped state label, a kind line, a title, an explanation, a collapsed for-the-record backstage strip, and action buttons
Repeated data-ui-ref attributes point to ui://inbox/build-review and related addresses
Explicitly omits any implemented marker on the six failure cards; only the happy path closes implemented
Frontstage/backstage separation is a stated law: the operator sees returned cards, not the CHECK→CLAIM→GATE→LAUNCH→VERIFY pipeline
Includes a No silent dead ends invariant banner and a panel tabbed inspector summarizing mechanisms
standards: The mockup is grounded in real source locations and event kinds rather than being a purely fictional UI
It names and visualizes six distinct non-closing outcomes: verify-fail, scope-overrun, FORM-unverifiable, launch-fail, cap-deferred, crashed-resurfaced
It makes frontstage-only display an explicit design rule, hiding the backstage state machine
It encodes the deny-all default for empty scope and the exactly-once / no-auto-relaunch rule for crashed builds
conventions: Follows the shared design-system.css variable naming and typography (Bricolage Grotesque, IBM Plex Mono)
Uses semantic data-ui-ref attributes to annotate UI addresses
Embeds a long, structured HTML comment for provenance and grounding
Breaks typical neutral mockup style by asserting implementation facts and code line references
Uses inline style attributes for small color swatches in the rail
concerns: Self-declared as a staging probe not yet registered or parsed, so it may be out of sync with canonical records
Line-number references in comments can drift as source files change
Multiple elements reuse the same data-ui-ref value, which may complicate precise address mapping
The for-the-record strip is described as collapsed but has no actual collapse behavior in the markup
No evidence of accessibility checks or responsive behavior beyond the desktop label
notes: A desktop mockup of the inbox view for six loud, recoverable build-return states, grounded in real backend event kinds and a frontstage-only rule.
questions: Is this mockup meant to be interactive or purely static documentation?
How do the card action buttons map to actual backend commands or events?
What is the exact relationship and transition between this D6 view and the C3 happy-path demonstrate-first card?
Will the data-ui-ref values be validated or resolved by another system?
What does the P2 probe status imply for acceptance and registration?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to show the six ways a dispatched build returns to the operator when it does not cleanly close; the file does exactly that through six cards plus explanatory rail and panel, matching the stated intent.
apparent_themes: Loud failure: every non-closing outcome must surface to the operator
Exactly-once semantics and no truncation for deferred or crashed work
Design review as a required gate for operator-facing surfaces
intention_signals: It is trying to design the operator-facing failure surface for a self-build pipeline, making non-happy-path outcomes visible, actionable, and traceable to real backend events while keeping the internal dispatch machinery off-stage.
novelty: The mockup doubles as a grounded specification by annotating each state with real source file line ranges and event kinds, and by making no silent dead ends an explicit frontstage invariant.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, named vocabulary and visual treatment for six non-closing build outcomes, plus the frontstage-only framing that keeps the operator inbox separate from backstage dispatch mechanics.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML/CSS desktop mockup that renders six loud, recoverable build-return cards at ui://inbox/build-review for a self-build pipeline, grounded in real backend event kinds and frontstage-only design.
intention_for_embedding: To specify the operator-facing inbox experience for non-happy-path build lifecycle states without exposing the backstage dispatch pipeline.
